papers.title: Characterization of participants in the Severe Asthma Research Program (SARP III)
papers.journal: American Journal of Respiratory and Critical Care Medicine
papers.access_route: BioLINCC application (biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov); SARP III specimens available via NHLBI BioLINCC
papers.notes: ICS, OCS, and biologic medication fully documented per subject; severe asthma focus; PBMC, serum, and plasma paired; multi-site US; n=714 with longitudinal visits

papers.title: SARP I and II: clinical features of severe asthma
papers.journal: American Journal of Respiratory and Critical Care Medicine
papers.notes: SARP I+II combined n=726; medication documented including ICS dose, OCS use, and controller regimen; sputum differential cell count available; serum IgE and eosinophil counts documented

papers.title: BREATHE: a large-scale multi-centre cohort study of adult asthma in Denmark and Sweden
papers.access_route: Application to BREATHE steering committee; contact via corresponding author at Copenhagen University Hospital
papers.notes: n=1403 adults; ICS, LABA, OCS, and biologic therapy documented; serum and plasma paired; Scandinavian population; registry linkage with national prescription databases for medication verification

notes: SARP I/II and SARP III draw from the same US multi-site program and share BioLINCC access. BREATHE provides the largest Scandinavian equivalent. All three have rigorous medication documentation — the key differentiator for this bundle.
